release_candidate_required_gates.evidence: go test ./... covers proxy/WebRTC policy, managed extra arg collision, profile lock, dry-run command generation, and wrapper/browser package behavior.
python3 -m unittest tests.test_detector_harness covers target listing, matrix planning, validation, sanitization rejection, operational timeout, and KG JSONL edges.
python3 -m unittest tests.test_package_runtime covers platform plan, missing browser rejection, wrapper/browser zip packaging and checksums.

BrowseForge commit 76ec639 dispatches browseforge-chromium through the Chromium launcher; local dogfood evidence records GET/runtime config, profile create, session launch, RuntimeID reporting, profile isolation, and Playwright Bind connection.

source-acquisition.json records checked-out Chromium refs/tags/150.0.7871.101, Linux deps sync, Docker deps image, GN output at out/BrowseForgeLinuxDocker, and applied BrowseForge scaffold/webdriver/hardware/screen/platform/timezone/locale/user-agent/storage-quota/plugins/WebRTC/audio/canvas patches.

release_candidate_required_gates.blocker: No patched Chromium binary exists; the only packaged artifact is a local dogfood bundle using installed Google Chrome 150.0.7871.49 and is explicitly not release-grade.

Source-level navigator.webdriver, navigator.hardwareConcurrency, navigator.deviceMemory, Screen API dimension, navigator.platform, timezone, navigator language, navigator.userAgent, navigator.userAgentData, and StorageManager quota and navigator.plugins/pdfViewerEnabled and WebRTC ICE candidate and WebAudio output and Canvas readback patches are applied in the external Chromium checkout, but no patched Chromium binary or live detector matrix exists yet; release-grade BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan/iphey/BrowserScan/CreepJS/SannySoft evidence remains required.
Dogfood package produced manifest/checksums/SBOM/provenance mechanics, but release-grade SBOM/provenance requires a real Chromium artifact plus Chromium DEPS snapshot/source checkout metadata.
